market.scope: Canadian partners, customers, market, currency, and outcomes only — no US persona or USD defaults.

tier1-leadership.label: Tier 1 Partner Leadership
tier1-leadership.who: Executives and VPs at large Cisco partners with an established security practice
tier1-leadership.tone: Board-level. Strategic, economic, concise. No feature talk.
tier1-leadership.lead_with: Market forces (regulation, insurance, market sorting), practice economics, PVI progression to Preferred status, investment alignment (MDF against named motions)
priorities: Bench capability as a scorecard driver
tier1-leadership.format_preferences: Short decks (8-12 slides), one idea per slide, declarative headers, a 'THE X —' kicker line per slide

t1-sellers.label: Tier 1 Partner Sellers (AMs & SEs)
t1-sellers.who: Account managers and sales engineers at established partners
t1-sellers.tone: Direct, tactical, punchy. What's in it for them, then how to run the motion.
t1-sellers.lead_with: Cisco Seller Rewards (personal payout), the five plays, talk tracks per motion, deal-reg habit, promotion math (TCF up to 85% off)
priorities: Deal registration before the customer call
avoid: PVI mechanics beyond what pays them
t1-sellers.format_preferences: Battlecard-style slides or one-pagers; objection/response tables; talk tracks verbatim

tier2-partners.label: Tier 2 / Distribution-Led Partners
tier2-partners.who: Canadian resellers and MSPs, multi-vendor bench — often network-led, building or expanding a security practice through disti
tier2-partners.tone: Respectful of their existing motion — 'not rebuilding, adding a layer'. Security is the door; one Cisco platform is the expansion.
tier2-partners.lead_with: Security door-openers first (audit, Risk Advisory, firewall seeding, identity attach) — then natural pull-through to networking refresh, data centre, and collaboration when the customer thread opens. Disti rep/SE as first call.
priorities: Platform thread: security trigger → network → identity → collab/DC when earned
PVI 1.0 deal-reg habit up to 5.0 rebate floor
tier2-partners.format_preferences: Playbook format (like From Quote to Practice): paths, plays, talk tracks, 'where it lands' examples with Canadian customer fits

disti-teams.label: Distributor Reps & SEs
disti-teams.who: Distribution sellers and engineers who carry the partner relationship
disti-teams.tone: Operational. Role clarity — what the rep carries vs what the SE carries.
disti-teams.lead_with: Rep: promotion math, Ignite Hunting/Teaming/Plus, PXP/DPV views, Cisco field intro timing. SE: pre-qualification/sizing, Secure Trial Console, dCloud, post-seed deployment support.
priorities: DPV visibility on PVI and motion velocity
Being the first call before Cisco field

customer-facing.label: Customer-Facing (via partners)
customer-facing.who: Canadian-headquartered mid-market organisations: healthcare, credit unions, school boards, manufacturers, Crown corps. Compliance load: PHIPA, PIPEDA, OSFI, FINTRAC.
customer-facing.tone: Vendor-agnostic, advisory, plain language. No product pitch in the room.
customer-facing.lead_with: Their posture, not products. Regulation and insurance pressure they already feel. Clear next step with a date on it.
customer-facing.format_preferences: Positioning cards, scored reports, 30-minute conversation formats

platform.headline: Security opens the door. One Cisco platform is the room.
platform.story_principle: Default inbound is security — lead there when the trigger is audit, breach, insurance, or identity. Stay security-only when the sponsor and budget are security-only. Expand across pillars when the customer names refresh, sprawl, hybrid pain, or AI plans. When the first conversation is networking, data centre, or collaboration — answer as SME on that pillar alone; pivot into the wider platform (including security) only when the thread opens.

security_only: Sponsor is CISO or security lead only
Deal is Qualified on a named security motion
Partner bench is security-only for this engagement
expand_from_security: Customer names network or campus refresh
pillar_first_standalone: Inbound is networking, DC, or collab RFP or renewal
Sponsor is network, infrastructure, apps, or collaboration lead
Deal registered on a non-security motion first
pillar_first_pivot_when: Segmentation, identity, or policy raised in a network call
Compliance, fraud, or data handling raised in collab
Workload isolation or shadow AI raised in DC
Refresh conversation touches firewall or zero trust

security.label: Security
security.sme_depth: Zero trust access, XDR, segmentation, cloud posture, identity enforcement
pivot_to_platform: Segmentation is a network architecture decision — thread to campus and DC when they name refresh
Identity spans collab and apps — thread when hybrid user pain surfaces

networking.label: Networking
attach_from_security: SNI branch or segmentation gaps → campus/branch refresh
Firewall as policy enforcement point → Catalyst/Meraki renewal
East-west visibility gaps → observability and ISE
Hybrid access pain → Secure Access on the same user base
pivot_to_platform: Refresh is the policy enforcement point — thread to Secure Firewall and segmentation when security joins the room
Campus identity and guest access → ISE and Duo without leading with 'security sale'
Same refresh budget may cover DC uplinks — thread when they mention data centre or AI plans
networking.sme_depth: Campus and branch, Catalyst and Meraki, Wi‑Fi, Hybrid Mesh Firewall, ThousandEyes

data-center.label: Data Centre
attach_from_security: Workload segmentation gaps → DC fabric and policy
Cloud consistency questions → Nexus and automation
AI/GPU plans exposing power, fabric, and east-west gaps
Splunk/XDR data gravity → observability in the DC
pivot_to_platform: East-west in the fabric is a security outcome — thread to segmentation and XDR when they name breach or compliance
AI workloads need policy isolation — thread to AI Defense and Secure Firewall without derailing DC sizing
Automation and observability compound with network refresh — thread when campus is in same programme
data-center.sme_depth: Nexus, UCS, Intersight, workload security, AI-ready infrastructure

collaboration.label: Collaboration
attach_from_security: Hybrid workforce security → Webex and calling security
Platform sprawl and shadow collab → consolidation story
Contact centre and customer trust → compliance-led collab refresh
Identity thread from Duo → single sign-on into collaboration
pivot_to_platform: External joiners and guest access are identity problems — thread to Duo when fraud or compliance surfaces
Contact centre recordings and PHI — thread to security and compliance without FUD
Same hybrid user base drives Secure Access — thread when VPN or app access pain appears in collab discovery
collaboration.sme_depth: Webex Suite, calling, contact centre, devices, hybrid meeting experience

threads.name: Audit → platform arc
threads.security_trigger: SNI audit, Risk Advisory, or insurance/compliance renewal
threads.arc: Scored audit → edge/segmentation gaps → network refresh → identity attach → optional collab/DC when customer names the pain
threads.partner_motion: Tier 2: disti SE sizes refresh; security funds the first conversation

threads.name: Refresh → security attach
threads.security_trigger: Network refresh RFP or Meraki/Catalyst renewal
threads.arc: Refresh in flight → policy enforcement and segmentation at the firewall → Secure Access and Duo on the same estate → XDR when SOC pain surfaces
threads.partner_motion: Partner-led network deal; security attach before close protects margin

threads.name: Identity as cross-pillar thread
threads.security_trigger: MFA gaps, stolen credential fear, or M365-only identity
threads.arc: Duo entry → Secure Access for app reach → ISE for campus → Webex identity for collab
threads.partner_motion: Lowest-friction SKU; compounds every other pillar

threads.name: AI readiness → DC + security
threads.security_trigger: AI adoption, GPU plans, or shadow AI discovery in audit
threads.arc: Shadow AI in audit → AI Defense → DC fabric and segmentation for workload isolation → observability
threads.partner_motion: Executive-led; disti brings DC SE when sizing matters

threads.name: Network-first → platform
threads.arc: Campus/branch refresh or Meraki/Catalyst renewal → policy enforcement and segmentation at the firewall → identity and Secure Access when access pain surfaces → optional collab/DC in same programme
threads.partner_motion: Answer the RFP as network SME first; disti SE owns sizing; security attach before close when policy or compliance enters

threads.name: DC-first → platform
threads.arc: Fabric, UCS, or AI-ready DC conversation → east-west and workload isolation → security segmentation and XDR when breach or compliance named → network campus if same capital cycle
threads.partner_motion: Lead with Intersight/Nexus/UCS depth; bring security when isolation or AI risk surfaces

threads.name: Collab-first → platform
threads.arc: Webex consolidation or contact centre refresh → hybrid experience and device estate → identity/Duo when guest access or fraud raised → Secure Access when same users hit app access pain
threads.partner_motion: Lead with collab economics and experience; thread security when compliance or identity enters

lifecycle.headline: ANALYZE → PLACE → LAND → ADOPT → EXPAND → RENEW

foundations.analyze: Leverage sales and customer data across the lifecycle
foundations.place: Allocate the right resources to the right transactions

messaging.content: The funded discovery motion. Turns a renewal quote into a strategic engagement. Door into every other play. Does not require customer budget — 30 minutes, scored output the customer keeps. Discovery question: "When is the last time you had an objective, third-party view of your security posture?"

Umbrella to Cisco Secure Access at the 50-seat threshold. Hybrid workforce demand is durable. Discovery question: "How are your users accessing apps today - VPN, ZTNA, a mix? What is working, what is breaking?"

Duo standalone or attached. Lowest-friction Cisco Secure SKU. The cross-sell that compounds every other motion. Layers cleanly on a Microsoft estate without displacement. Discovery question: "60% of breaches start with identity. What are you doing beyond MFA?"

Cisco places a Secure Firewall at low or no upfront cost. Targeted Competitive Firewall up to 85% off displacement. Hardware margin on conversion, recurring on Security Cloud Control attach. No user-count gate — works at any size. Pull-through into Identity and Segmentation. Sequence: hardware install, then SCC attach, then Duo, then segmentation. Discovery question: "How old is your firewall estate? When did you last do a rule hygiene review?"

Network segmentation extended into a security motion. The firewall becomes the policy enforcement point. Builds on existing ISE deployments. Discovery question: "If an attacker got into your network today, how far could they move before you would see it?"

Cloud-managed networking partner. Extend the install base into XDR (network-only customer becomes XDR-attached), Duo on the same user base, Umbrella-to-Secure-Access at 50 seats. Customer fit: multi-site retailer 30-200 locations, healthcare network, school district, property-management portfolio.

Enterprise networking partner with Catalyst, ISE, aging ASA. ASA-to-Secure-Firewall migration with TCF applied, segmentation on existing ISE, Cloud Protection Suite for off-prem workloads. Renewal motion, not displacement. Customer fit: regional credit union or insurer, provincial Crown corporation, manufacturer with multiple plants, higher-ed campus.

No significant Cisco Secure footprint yet. Lead with Risk Advisory, build the bench on those engagements, identify 2-3 firewall seeding candidates. Black Belt, FireJumper SE, Partner Journeys as the structured path. Arc: two funded engagements Q1, one displacement Q2, named Cisco Secure brand by Q4. Longest motion, compounds hardest.

The lock-in play. Cisco wins: Hybrid Mesh Firewall consolidates cloud, on-prem, and hybrid without the proprietary fabric tax. TCF at up to 85% off. Security Cloud Control as the unified management plane.

Premium pricing, Canadian budget. Cisco lands where Palo prices out: equivalent capability (Secure Firewall + SCC + Duo + XDR) sized for the budget the customer actually has. The TCO conversation is decisive.
Cost-first and installed. Sophos: win when the customer outgrows the managed-service model. Checkpoint: win at the refresh cycle with TCF pricing. Integration story is the differentiator.
The non-vendor threat. Not a feature comparison — a risk-advisory conversation. Duo layers cleanly on the Microsoft estate without displacement. Gaps are exposed, not argued.

Federal legislation moving through Parliament will require critical infrastructure operators to have documented security programmes. OSFI already mandates identity controls for federally regulated financial institutions. The expectation is spreading beyond finance. Compliance context for Canadian buyers: PHIPA, PIPEDA, OSFI, FINTRAC.
At renewal — not at sale — carriers demand evidence of security controls. Most customers don't have the documentation. The partner who helps them get there owns the relationship for a long time.

The market is sorting partners into two groups: those who can lead a security conversation own the account; those who can't are quoting on price against partners with better buying power. The gap is widening and doesn't close on its own.

Points and cash deposited directly to the individual seller's wallet on eligible Cisco Secure deals. AMs and SEs qualify. Redeemable for gift cards, merchandise, experiences. The reward is the seller's, not the company's discretionary bonus. Enrol: cisco.com/go/sellerrewards.

PVI 1.0: deal-reg eligibility (Hunting and Teaming registrations; build one registered motion per quarter). PVI 5.0: rebate floor (build a Black Belt cohort per quarter). PVI 7.5+: Preferred Partner status, Topology Builder, dCloud eXpo, higher rebate tiers (hold quarterly business reviews with Cisco field). Capabilities is the fastest dimension to move on the scorecard.

Partners with 60%+ Black Belt certification post 10% higher security booking growth. Black Belt cohorts and FireJumper SE accreditation lift the bench; Topology Builder and dCloud eXpo give SEs production-grade demos without a lab build.

This is not a risk assessment — it is a network architecture readiness check. It asks a different set of questions about a different layer of the stack, and connects directly to an improvement path. Complementary, not competitive.

messaging.topic: Is this just a Cisco pitch in disguise?
messaging.content: The audit is vendor-agnostic — 10 questions about your posture, not about products. The scored output is yours to keep regardless of what happens next. If Cisco solutions are relevant, we will be transparent about that in the follow-on conversation.

messaging.topic: Our current vendor already does this
messaging.content: Did the output of that assessment produce a clear next step, and are you on track to act on it? Most of the time the answer is no. That is the gap this motion closes — from insight to implementation, not just insight.

messaging.topic: We don't have budget right now
messaging.content: The audit does not require budget. It is 30 minutes and produces a scored report you keep. It builds the business case for when budget does appear — and tells you which gap to address first.

messaging.topic: We just renewed our security contracts
messaging.content: Renewal is the best time — it gives you a baseline to measure against at the next cycle, and may surface areas your current vendor is not covering before the next renewal.
Three aging Fortinets at refresh. TCO analysis showed a 40% five-year saving with Secure Firewall.
1100 series seeded at a school board's Internet edge. Thirty days of real traffic — at refresh, it stays.
Managed firewall service: monthly fee replaces the capital event. Five-year contract.
Network team sees cloud, on-prem, and OT in one console. The Fortinet conversation ends there.

WEEKLY (bench): pipeline scrub (3 deals minimum), deal-reg review before customer calls, Trial Console check with SE on at-risk trials by Friday. MONTHLY (practice): Black Belt completion review, PVI score read (PXP partner-side, DPV disti-side — same numbers both sides), 3-month pipeline coverage check. QUARTERLY (leadership): joint Cisco/disti/partner plan review, MDF alignment against named motions, Black Belt / FireJumper cohort planning.

DISTI REP carries: promotion math (TCF, Ignite Hunting/Teaming/Plus, XDR Trial), PXP/DPV data views, Cisco partner-team intro when the deal is real. DISTI SE carries: pre-qualification (sizing, user counts, licensing — saves the partner from quoting wrong), trial/demo plumbing (Secure Trial Console, dCloud), post-seed deployment through conversion. The post-seed handoff is where deals close or stall.

Tier 1 runs EIGHT plays: Risk Advisory, Zero Trust Access, AI, Cloud, Identity, Secure Networking, Segmentation, SOC. Tier 2 runs SIX: the core five (Risk Advisory, Zero Trust Access, Identity, Secure Networking, Segmentation) plus Cloud. AI and SOC are Tier 1 plays.

Consistent security policy across AWS, Azure, and GCP. Cloud Protection Suite for workloads that have moved off-prem. Discovery question: "How consistent is your security policy across AWS, Azure, and GCP today?"

Lead with AI Defense for shadow AI visibility; expand into Hypershield + Secure Workload. Natural co-sell with DC / AI Pod teams. Discovery question: "Which AI tools are your employees using that you do not have visibility into?"

SOC modernisation - replace or augment legacy SIEM with XDR + Splunk ES. Often rides on a Splunk renewal or cyber insurance cycle. Discovery question: "What is your MTTD and MTTR today? How does it compare to where you want to be?"

Network & firewall refresh: modernise aging estates onto FTD 10.0 / Hybrid Mesh Firewall; often rides on Catalyst or Meraki refresh. Typical ACV CAD 150K–2M, 90-180 days. Assets: firewall rule hygiene, migration workshop, virtual-patching demo. The label matters - it tells the Cisco AM what support to line up.

Cross-pillar ZTA + Segmentation: Zero Trust Access + Secure Networking + Segmentation combined (SSE + ISE + Firewall + Secure Workload). Typical ACV CAD 500K–5M, 180-270 days. Assets: Universal ZTA session, identity/network/workload threading, EA consolidation.

messaging.topic: Named motion - Breach Protection by XDR
messaging.content: SOC modernisation: replace or augment legacy SIEM with XDR + Splunk ES; rides on Splunk renewal or cyber insurance cycle. Typical ACV CAD 200K–3M, 90-150 days. Assets: SOC maturity assessment, Splunk data onboarding, XDR CTF hands-on.

AI Defense + Hypershield + Secure AI Factory. Lead with AI Defense for shadow AI visibility, expand into Hypershield + Secure Workload; co-sell with DC / AI Pod teams. Typical ACV CAD 300K–5M, 120-240 days. Assets: shadow AI exposure assessment, AI risk posture workshop, Secure AI Factory review.

1) What is the trigger? (breach/incident, insurance renewal, new CISO, regulatory change, EA renewal, cloud/AI rollout, firewall/EDR EOL - no compelling event, no urgency, deal stalls). 2) What is the outcome? (reduce MTTD/MTTR, pass audit, cut premium, consolidate tools, protect AI adoption, enable hybrid work, stop ransomware - lead with outcome, sell the suite, attach products). 3) Who decides and signs? (map the buying committee: champion, technical buyer, economic buyer, influencer, user, procurement - identify three minimum).

10% EARLY: named sponsor, trigger identified, compelling event being validated. 25% QUALIFIED: discovery done, budget indicator, decision process mapped - pipeline-eligible. 50% EVALUATION: PoV/demo underway, economic buyer engaged - best-case territory. 75% NEGOTIATION: pricing agreed, paper in flight - commit when close date inside 30 days and blocker named. 90% CLOSE: signed SOW/PO - expansion and attach motion opens here.

They're defending a past purchase; they want proof migration or coexistence is worth it. Reframe: "We do not have to rip anything out. We can coexist and prove value at the margins - cloud, AI, or identity - and let the numbers decide."

messaging.topic: Microsoft E5 already gives me identity and security
messaging.content: Economic buyer optimising for bundling; security buyer often sceptical of E5 depth. Reframe: "E5 is great at Microsoft-centric identity. 60% of breaches involve identity - attackers do not stop at Microsoft. Talos sees it all."

messaging.topic: No budget for a new security investment (enterprise)
messaging.content: They have not tied the investment to a compelling event. Anchor to the trigger - breach, renewal, audit, insurance: "What is the cost if X happens? Let us size the investment against that."

messaging.topic: We are consolidating vendors, not adding one
messaging.content: Actually an OPPORTUNITY - Cisco replaces four to six point vendors. Reframe: "We agree. Let us list your current vendors. I will show you which ones we replace and what it saves you."

messaging.topic: Your pricing is higher than competitor
messaging.content: Early-cycle signal; they have not seen full TCO. Pivot to TCO + consolidation savings + rebates: "Sticker price differs. Three-year TCO - let us do the math together."

messaging.topic: We tried Cisco years ago and it did not work out
messaging.content: Legitimate legacy objection (old Umbrella, ISE 2.x, old Firepower). Reframe: "Here is what is materially different: new architecture, new UX, new ecosystem. Let us do a 90-day pilot and you decide."

Strength: firewall share leader, strong SSE (Prisma Access), platform narrative. Weakness: expensive; Prisma Access vs Cloud vs SASE line confusion; weak in identity. Cisco angle: only vendor unifying network + security + identity + AI in ONE architecture - HMF + SSE + ISE + Splunk is a story Palo cannot match; TCO favours Cisco in multi-product deals.

Strength: best-in-class EDR/XDR, strong brand, tight Falcon platform. Weakness: endpoint-first (network telemetry gap), nascent SIEM story, single-point-of-failure concern after 2024. Cisco angle: XDR + Splunk delivers SIEM depth with network + endpoint + email + identity breadth CrowdStrike cannot match. Do not fight on endpoint - fight on correlation depth.

Strength: E5 bundling, Microsoft-graph integration, CFO economics. Weakness: Microsoft-centric coverage - non-M365 identity, non-Azure cloud, non-Windows endpoint are thin; vendor concentration risk. Cisco angle: "Coexistence, not replacement." Cisco + Microsoft is the most common security architecture; Cisco brings Talos intel, multi-cloud coverage, network + identity depth.

dCloud (self-service demo labs, dcloud.cisco.com - Secure Access, Duo, ISE, XDR, Firewall). Secure Trials Console (30-day trials via PAM). CPOC (hardware PoV on-prem 30-90 days via TSA, qualified customer + success criteria required). CXC executive briefing centres (Toronto, NYC, San Jose, RTP - book via PAM). Talos briefing (CISO-level door-opener, not a closer). XDR/Identity CTF (live attack simulations - best enablement tool available). Never promise what you cannot deliver.

Demo this week: dCloud, then Cisco SE (48h). Technical blocker: Security TSA via PAM (48h). Pricing/packaging: Security Sales Specialist via PAM (48h). Competitive escalation: Competitive Team via PAM (this week). Active incident: Talos IR urgent number from PAM (same day - stop all pipeline thinking). Specialisation blocker: PAM + Partner Development Manager (this week).

1) Five+ discovery calls with a security trigger question. 2) At least one deal moves a stage (30+ days stuck = quietly slipping). 3) Demo or TSA session booked for a Qualified+ deal (demos shorten cycles 30-50%). 4) New opportunity registered in Cisco Commerce Workplace at Qualified - not later. 5) Forecast reflects reality (no sandbagging, no over-calling). 6) PAM sync with top three deals named and specific asks. 7) Thirty minutes in Black Belt or Fire Jumper - the skill edge compounds.

THE DOOR — Security is how they already think about you: audit, breach pressure, insurance, identity, refresh at the edge. THE ROOM — One architecture story: network as enforcement point, security as visibility and response, collaboration and DC as workloads that inherit the same policy. THE RULE — Never pivot to networking or collab because the slide deck has a section; pivot when the customer names refresh, sprawl, hybrid pain, or AI plans.

Stay deep on security when: the sponsor is CISO or security lead only; budget is security line-item; partner bench is security-only; deal is Qualified on a named security motion. Your job is SME depth — XDR correlation, ZTNA posture, segmentation design — not platform breadth for its own sake.

messaging.topic: When the first call is not security
messaging.content: Networking, DC, or collab inbound: answer as SME on THAT pillar first — sizing, architecture, economics, disti SE role. Do not force security opener. Pivot to platform (including security) only when: segmentation/identity/policy appears in network; isolation/compliance/AI risk in DC; guest access/fraud/hybrid access in collab. THE RETURN — one sentence tying pillars together, not a second pitch.

After answering refresh/Wi‑Fi/SD-WAN as network SME: 'The same refresh is where segmentation policy lives — worth one conversation with security before PO.' 'If hybrid access is on the pain list, Secure Access often lands on the same user base.' 'DC uplinks and AI plans in the same programme? That is a fabric conversation — we can thread when you are ready.' Stop if they close the door.

After UCS/Nexus/AI-ready depth: 'East-west in the fabric is the isolation question security teams ask at audit — we can align policy if that is on your calendar.' 'GPU workloads without segmentation is the shadow-AI conversation in another language.' 'Campus refresh in the same capital cycle? Network and DC compound on one architecture call.'

After Webex/calling/contact centre SME work: 'Guest joiners and external meetings are an identity conversation — Duo threads without a separate security project.' 'PHI or recordings in contact centre? That is compliance language security buyers already use.' 'Same hybrid users hitting VPN for apps? Secure Access often follows collab consolidation.'

Three modes: (1) SECURITY-ONLY — CISO-only, security budget, qualified security motion. (2) SECURITY-LED PLATFORM — default inbound; expand on refresh/sprawl/hybrid/AI signals. (3) PILLAR-FIRST — network/DC/collab inbound; SME answer standalone; optional platform pivot when routing.pillar_first_pivot_when signals appear. Never catalogue-sell across pillars in one breath.

Natural pivots after security discovery: 'The firewall you are refreshing is where segmentation policy lives — that is a network conversation with a security outcome.' 'Your SNI branch score says edge hygiene; that is campus and Meraki/Catalyst refresh timing, not a SKU dump.' 'East-west visibility is a network telemetry problem with a security consequence.'

Natural pivots: 'When AI workloads land, the question is not GPU count first — it is who can move laterally in the fabric.' 'Cloud policy gaps you flagged in audit become DC and automation conversations when workloads repatriate or burst.' 'Splunk and XDR data gravity pulls observability into the DC — same team, same budget cycle.'

Natural pivots: 'Hybrid access pain is the same user whether they reach an app or a meeting.' 'Three meeting platforms is a shadow-IT story — same discovery motion as SaaS visibility in audit.' 'Contact centre fraud and customer trust are security outcomes with a Webex buying centre.'

Attach from security refresh or SNI edge gaps. Catalyst and Meraki renewal with firewall as policy point. Discovery question: 'When does your network refresh land relative to your security audit findings?' Disti SE carries sizing; partner carries install relationship.

Attach from AI plans, GPU projects, or segmentation gaps in audit. Nexus and automation for consistent policy; workload isolation as security outcome. Discovery question: 'Where will inference workloads live — and who owns east-west policy when they do?'

Attach from hybrid workforce security and SaaS sprawl findings. Calling, meetings, and devices under one policy story — identity from Duo threads cleanly. Discovery question: 'How many meeting and calling platforms are you paying for — and who can join from outside without your security team knowing?'

style-guide.text: Write in Brandon Randell's established voice (Partner Security Account Executive expanding portfolio, Cisco Canada): Declarative, short sentences. Sentence-case headers that end with a period. Em-dash kickers: 'THE RULE — ...', 'THE COMPOUND — ...', 'THE THREAD — ...'. LEAD WITH SECURITY — audit, breach pressure, insurance, identity, edge refresh — because that is how customers and Tier 2 partners find you. THEN COMPOUND into one Cisco platform only when the thread is natural: network as enforcement, DC as workload destination, collab as the same hybrid user. Never catalogue-sell; thread-sell. Core vocabulary: motion, practice, play, bench, compound, seed, door-opener, pull-through, platform thread. Canadian English, CAD, Canadian verticals (credit unions, school boards, Crown corps, PHIPA/PIPEDA/OSFI). Insider voice: market force first, product second. Footer: 'Cisco Canada Partner Growth · FY26' + series name.
style-guide.notes: Security-led, platform-capable. Lead with the door; expand to the room when earned.

deck-from-master.text: Adapt master content into a slide deck: One idea per slide. Slide header is a declarative sentence with a period. Body in 2-3 labelled columns (e.g. 'SELLING TODAY / EXTEND INTO / CUSTOMER FIT'), each column 2-4 short paragraphs max. Close each slide with an em-dash kicker line ('THE SEQUENCE — ...'). 8-14 slides total. Page footer with series name and page numbers. Cut anything the target audience wouldn't act on (see audience profile 'avoid' list). Leadership gets economics and cadence; sellers get talk tracks and incentives; tier 2 gets entry motions and disti support.

one-pager.text: Positioning-card format (like 'why-this-audit'): Title as three fragments ('WHY THIS AUDIT. WHY NOW. WHY US.'). Open with 'The one-sentence answer'. Then 'Three things that make this different' as a numbered table. Then objection/response table ('WHAT THEY SAY' / 'HOW TO RESPOND') — pull pairs from the messaging library, category=objection. Close with the plain-language investment case and a bulleted 'what you get' list. Footer: series name + confidentiality line.

playbook.text: Playbook format (like 'From Quote to Practice'): open with who the partner is / who the customer is / why now (three market forces). Security door-opener first. Then one Cisco platform thread (security → network → identity → collab/DC when earned). Reward stack (seller / bench / practice). Plays by pillar — security plays numbered first, pull-through plays labelled NETWORK / DC / COLLAB. Paths (A/B/C) with SELLING TODAY / EXTEND INTO / CUSTOMER FIT. Competitive section. Close with PVI, disti role split, platform compound call to action. 12-16 pages.

platform-story.text: Structure every security-led platform narrative in four beats: 1) THE TRIGGER — what the customer already feels (audit score, breach, insurance, refresh deadline). 2) THE SECURITY OUTCOME — what good looks like in 90 days (visibility, access, segmentation). 3) THE PLATFORM THREAD — one sentence per pillar only if the customer or partner opened the door (network refresh, DC/AI, collab sprawl). 4) THE NEXT STEP — one dated action, one owner. Do not list SKUs; list architecture decisions. Close with THE COMPOUND — how each pillar makes the next cheaper to adopt.
platform-story.notes: Default narrative mode for Tier 2 disti-led motions. Use with build_platform_brief.

pillar-deep-dive.text: When going deep on one pillar (security, networking, data-center, collaboration): open with one-line tie-back to the security trigger that earned the conversation. Then SME depth — architecture patterns, discovery questions, disti SE vs partner roles, proof points, objections for THAT pillar only. Do not re-pitch other pillars unless the customer asks. Close with THE RETURN — how this pillar work strengthens the platform story already in flight.
pillar-deep-dive.notes: Use when sponsor or deal is single-pillar but security opened the door.

pillar-first.text: When the first conversation is networking, data centre, or collaboration — not security: 1) THE ASK — answer their RFP, refresh, or sizing question as SME on THAT pillar. No forced security opener. 2) SME DEPTH — architecture, economics, disti SE vs partner roles, objections for that pillar only. 3) OPTIONAL PIVOT — one sentence toward platform (including security) ONLY if routing.pillar_first_pivot_when signals appear; use pivot_to_platform lines from the pillar profile. 4) THE NEXT STEP — one dated action on their pillar. Close with THE RETURN only if you pivoted — one line tying pillars, not a second pitch.

security-only.text: Stay security-only when routing.security_only applies: CISO-only sponsor, security line-item budget, or Qualified on a named security motion. Structure: 1) THE TRIGGER — audit, breach, insurance, compliance. 2) THE SECURITY OUTCOME — visibility, access, segmentation in 90 days. 3) SME DEPTH — XDR, ZTNA, firewall, identity for THIS deal only. Do NOT introduce networking, DC, or collab pull-through. Close with one security next step and owner.
